panels.description:
Load balancing at Layer 4: total number of TCP and UDP connections from the monitored object to backend servers

Load balancing at Layer 7: total number of TCP connections from the clients to the monitored object

Unit: Count
panels.title: Concurrent Connections

panels.description:
Number of TCP and UDP connections in the **ESTABLISHED** state between the monitored object and backend servers

You can run the following command to view the connections (both Windows and Linux servers):

.. code-block::

netstat -an

Unit: Count
panels.title: Active Connections

panels.description:
Number of TCP connections between the monitored object and backend servers except those in the **ESTABLISHED** state

You can run the following command to view the connections (both Windows and Linux servers):

.. code-block::

netstat -an

Unit: Count
panels.title: Inactive Connections

panels.description:
Number of TCP and UDP connections established between clients and the monitored object per second

Unit: piece
panels.title: New Connections

panels.description:
Number of packets received by the monitored object per second

Unit: piece
panels.title: Incoming Packets

panels.description:
Number of packets sent from the monitored object per second

Unit: piece
panels.title: Outgoing Packets

panels.description:
Traffic used for accessing the monitored object from the Internet per second

Unit: byte/s
panels.title: Inbound Rate

panels.description:
Traffic used by the monitored object to access the Internet per second

Unit: byte/s
panels.title: Outbound Rate

panels.description:
Number of unhealthy backend servers associated with the monitored object

Unit: Count
panels.title: Unhealthy Servers

panels.description:
Number of healthy backend servers associated with the monitored object

Unit: Count
panels.title: Healthy Servers

panels.description:
(TCP listener metrics) Number of reset packets forwarded by the monitored object from backend servers to clients

Unit: Count/s
panels.title: Reset Packets from Backend Servers

panels.description:
(TCP listener metrics) Number of reset packets forwarded by the monitored object from clients to backend servers

Unit: Count/s
panels.title: Reset Packets from Clients

panels.description:
(TCP listener metrics) Number of reset packets generated by the monitored object per second

Unit: Packet/s
panels.title: Reset Packets from Load Balancers

panels.description:
Bandwidth used for accessing the monitored object from the Internet

Unit: bit/s
panels.title: Inbound Bandwidth

panels.description:
Bandwidth used by the monitored object to access the Internet

Unit: bit/s
panels.title: Outbound Bandwidth

panels.description:
Number of requests the monitored object receives per second (This metric is available only when the frontend protocol is HTTP or HTTPS.)

Unit: Count/s
panels.title: Layer-7 Query Rate

panels.description:
Number of 3xx status codes returned by the monitored object (This metric is available only when the frontend protocol is HTTP or HTTPS.)

Unit: Count/s
panels.title: Layer-7 3xx Status Codes

panels.description:
Number of 2xx status codes returned by the monitored object (This metric is available only when the frontend protocol is HTTP or HTTPS.)

Unit: Count/s
panels.title: 2xx Status Codes

panels.description:
Number of 4xx status codes returned by the monitored object (This metric is available only when the frontend protocol is HTTP or HTTPS.)

Unit: Count/s
panels.title: Layer-7 4xx Status Codes

panels.description:
Number of 5xx status codes returned by the monitored object (This metric is available only when the frontend protocol is HTTP or HTTPS.)

Unit: Count/s
panels.title: Layer-7 5xx Status Codes

panels.description:
Number of status codes returned by the monitored object except 2xx, 3xx, 4xx, and 5xx status codes (This metric is available only when the frontend protocol is HTTP or HTTPS.)

Unit: Count/s
panels.title: Layer-7 Other Status Codes

panels.description:
Number of 404 Not Found status codes returned by the monitored object (This metric is available only when the frontend protocol is HTTP or HTTPS.)

Unit: Count/s
panels.title: Layer-7 404 Not Found

panels.description:
Number of 499 Client Closed Request status codes returned by the monitored object (This metric is available only when the frontend protocol is HTTP or HTTPS.)

Unit: Count/s
panels.title: Layer-7 499 Client Closed Request

panels.description:
Number of 502 Bad Gateway status codes returned by the monitored object (This metric is available only when the frontend protocol is HTTP or HTTPS.)

Unit: Count/s
panels.title: Layer-7 502 Bad Gateway

panels.description:
Average response time of the monitored object (This metric is available only when the frontend protocol is HTTP or HTTPS.)

The response time starts when the monitored object receives requests from the clients and ends when it returns all responses to the clients.

Unit: ms



**Note:**

The average response time it takes to establish a WebSocket connection may be very long. This metric cannot be used as a reference.
panels.title: Average Layer-7 Response Time

panels.description:
Number of 4xx status codes returned by the monitored object (This metric is available only when the frontend protocol is HTTP or HTTPS.)

Unit: Count/s
panels.title: 4xx Status Codes Backend

panels.description:
Number of 5xx status codes returned by the monitored object

(This metric is available only when the frontend protocol is HTTP or HTTPS.)

Unit: Count/s
panels.title: 5xx Status Codes Backend

panels.description:
Average response time of backend servers (This metric is available only when the frontend protocol is HTTP or HTTPS.)

The response time starts when the monitored object routes the requests to the backend server and ends when the monitored object receives a response from the backend server.

Unit: ms



**Note:**

The average response time it takes to establish a WebSocket connection may be very long. This metric cannot be used as a reference.
panels.title: Average Server Response Time

panels.description:
Maximum response time of backend servers (This metric is available only when the frontend protocol is HTTP or HTTPS.)

The response time starts when the monitored object routes the requests to the backend server and ends when the monitored object receives a response from the backend server.

Unit: ms
panels.title: Maximum Server Response Time

panels.description:
Maximum response time of backend servers (This metric is available only when the frontend protocol is HTTP or HTTPS.)

The response time starts when the monitored object routes the requests to the backend server and ends when the monitored object receives a response from the backend server.

Unit: ms
panels.title: Minimum Server Response Time

panels.description:
Maximum response time of the monitored object. (This metric is available only when the frontend protocol is HTTP or HTTPS.)

The response time starts when the monitored object receives requests from the clients and ends when it returns all responses to the clients.

Unit: ms
panels.title: Maximum Layer-7 Response Time

panels.description:
Minimum response time of the monitored object. (This metric is available only when the frontend protocol is HTTP or HTTPS.)

The response time starts when the monitored object receives requests from the clients and ends when it returns all responses to the clients.

Unit: ms
panels.title: Minimum Layer-7 Response Time

panels.description:
Ratio of HTTP and HTTPS connections established between the monitored object and backend servers per second, to the maximum number of concurrent connections allowed per second

Unit: percent (%)
panels.title: Layer-7 Concurrent Connection Usage

panels.description:
Ratio of the bandwidth that the monitored object uses to receive requests from clients over HTTP and HTTPS, to the maximum inbound bandwidth allowed

Unit: percent (%)

.. caution::

CAUTION: If the inbound bandwidth usage reaches 100%, the load balancer performance has reached the upper limit. If the inbound bandwidth keeps higher than the bandwidth that the load balancer can provide, the service availability cannot be guaranteed.
panels.title: Layer-7 Inbound Bandwidth Usage

panels.description:
Ratio of the bandwidth that the monitored object uses to return response to clients over HTTP and HTTPS, to the maximum outbound bandwidth allowed

Unit: percent (%)

.. caution::

CAUTION: If the outbound bandwidth usage reaches 100%, the load balancer performance has reached the upper limit. If the outbound bandwidth keeps higher than the bandwidth that the load balancer can provide, the service availability cannot be guaranteed.
panels.title: Layer-7 Outbound Bandwidth Usage

panels.description:
Ratio of HTTP and HTTPS connections established between clients and the monitored object per second, to the maximum number of new connections allowed per second

Unit: percent (%)
panels.title: Layer-7 New Connection Usage

panels.description:
Ratio of HTTP and HTTPS queries per second on the monitored object, to the maximum number of queries allowed per second

Unit: percent (%)
panels.title: Layer 7 QPS Usage

panels.description:
Ratio of TCP and UDP connections established between the monitored object and backend servers per second, to the maximum number of concurrent connections allowed per second

Unit: percent (%)
panels.title: Layer-4 Concurrent Connection Usage

panels.description:
Ratio of the bandwidth that the monitored object uses to receive requests from clients over TCP and UDP, to the maximum inbound bandwidth allowed

Unit: percent (%)

.. caution::

CAUTION: If the inbound bandwidth usage reaches 100%, the load balancer performance has reached the upper limit. If the inbound bandwidth keeps higher than the bandwidth that the load balancer can provide, the service availability cannot be guaranteed.
panels.title: Layer-4 Inbound Bandwidth Usage

panels.description:
Ratio of the bandwidth that the monitored object uses to return response to clients over TCP and UDP, to the maximum outbound bandwidth allowed

Unit: percent (%)

.. caution::

CAUTION: If the outbound bandwidth usage reaches 100%, the load balancer performance has reached the upper limit. If the outbound bandwidth keeps higher than the bandwidth that the load balancer can provide, the service availability cannot be guaranteed.
panels.title: Layer-4 Outbound Bandwidth Usage

panels.description:
Ratio of TCP and UDP connections established between clients and the monitored object per second, to the maximum number of new connections allowed per second

Unit: percent (%)
panels.title: Layer-4 New Connection Usage

list.description: Datasource where the OTC Prometheus Exporter data is stored
list.label: Datasource
list.name: datasource
